comment_text.sn36.3:1.4: The feelings themselves are inherent to the functioning of the mind, so they remain so long as the mind remains. But how we respond to feelings is conditioned by underlying tendencies, which can be abandoned.

root_text.sn36.3:1.3: Sukhā vedanā, dukkhā vedanā, adukkhamasukhā vedanā.
root_text.sn36.3:1.4: Sukhāya, bhikkhave, vedanāya rāgānusayo pahātabbo, dukkhāya vedanāya paṭighānusayo pahātabbo, adukkhamasukhāya vedanāya avijjānusayo pahātabbo.
root_text.sn36.3:1.5: Yato kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno sukhāya vedanāya rāgānusayo pahīno hoti, dukkhāya vedanāya paṭighānusayo pahīno hoti, adukkhamasukhāya vedanāya avijjānusayo pahīno hoti, ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘bhikkhu niranusayo sammaddaso acchecchi taṇhaṁ, vivattayi saṁyojanaṁ, sammā mānābhisamayā antamakāsi dukkhassā’ti.

translation_text.sn36.3:1.4: The underlying tendency to greed should be given up when it comes to pleasant feeling. The underlying tendency to repulsion should be given up when it comes to painful feeling. The underlying tendency to ignorance should be given up when it comes to neutral feeling.
translation_text.sn36.3:1.5: When a mendicant has given up these underlying tendencies, they’re called a mendicant without underlying tendencies, who sees rightly, has cut off craving, cast off the fetters, and by rightly comprehending conceit has made an end of suffering.
translation_text.sn36.3:2.3: the underlying tendency to greed is there,
translation_text.sn36.3:2.4: if you don’t see the escape.
translation_text.sn36.3:3.3: the underlying tendency to repulsion is there,
translation_text.sn36.3:3.4: if you don’t see the escape.
translation_text.sn36.3:4.1: As for that peaceful, neutral feeling:
translation_text.sn36.3:4.2: he of vast wisdom has taught
translation_text.sn36.3:4.4: you’re still not released from suffering.
translation_text.sn36.3:5.1: But when a mendicant is keen,
translation_text.sn36.3:6.2: they’re without defilements in this very life.
translation_text.sn36.3:6.3: That knowledge master is firm in principle;
translation_text.sn36.3:6.4: when their body breaks up, they can’t be reckoned.”
